statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 17 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, driven by Middle East supply disruptions that have pushed oil prices up 31% since February.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as Middle East supply disruptions continue to impact regional stockpiles. The EU average diesel reserves of 88.8 days have fallen below the mandatory 90-day threshold, while petrol (96.3 days) and jet fuel (87.3 days) remain concerningly close to minimum requirements. This deterioration follows strategic reserve releases by multiple countries responding to supply chain disruptions.

The strain is unevenly distributed across the bloc. Eastern European nations show particular vulnerability, with Poland, Austria, Romania, and Luxembourg all maintaining critical levels across multiple fuel types. Romania's diesel reserves at just 30.2 days represent the most severe shortage, while Poland's jet fuel stocks at 22.5 days highlight aviation fuel vulnerabilities. Conversely, Nordic and Baltic states like Estonia, Latvia, and Finland maintain robust reserves, with some countries holding over 200 days of certain fuels.

Fuel prices reflect the underlying supply pressures, with Brent crude at $94.74 per barrel representing a 31% increase since the February escalation. Average EU petrol prices of €1.812 per liter and diesel at €1.821 per liter show significant regional variation, from Malta's relatively low €1.34 petrol to Denmark's €2.392. This price disparity, combined with uneven stockpile distributions, suggests continued market stress as the bloc manages both immediate supply security and longer-term strategic positioning.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries maintain critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Eastern Europe particularly vulnerable
EU diesel reserves at 88.8 days have fallen below the mandatory 90-day minimum threshold
Romania faces the most severe shortage with diesel reserves at just 30.2 days, while Poland's jet fuel stocks sit at critically low 22.5 days
Oil prices have surged 31% since February's Middle East escalation, with Brent crude now at $94.74 per barrel
Regional price variations span from Malta's €1.34 petrol to Denmark's €2.392, reflecting uneven supply pressures across the bloc
